title: DelegateManager.claimRewards allows unauthorized reward claims
reason: Caller control is missing, but the function does not redirect rewards to the caller; rewards are credited to the specified service provider and delegators. Reconstructed separately as a grief-only zero-value claim issue, not theft.

title: Guardian can execute arbitrary transactions without voting
reason: This is an explicit guardian privilege in the protocol design, not a bug in the implemented access control.

title: Initializable initializer modifier blocks contract initialization
reason: The initializer checks `proxyAdmin` in proxy storage; this code alone does not show initialization is impossible, and the proxy pattern is intended to supply that slot.

title: Registry has single point of failure with onlyOwner access
reason: This is a governance/trust-model centralization concern rather than a distinct implementation vulnerability.

title: ServiceProviderFactory _validateBalanceInternal missing deployerStake validation
reason: Rejected as stated: registration validates current deployer stake against the minimum, and `decreaseStake()` revalidates after updating `deployerStake`, so the claimed bypass is not supported.

title: ClaimsManager processClaim potential division by zero
reason: Too speculative as a standalone reportable issue here; reaching `totalStakedAt(fundedBlock) == 0` for a claimable provider depends on zero-minimum service-type configuration, and no distinct persistent harmful state beyond that edge case is demonstrated.

title: ServiceProviderFactory allows registration with zero stake
reason: Not unconditional: `register()` immediately calls stake-balance validation, so zero-stake registration only succeeds if governance created a service type with `minStake == 0`.

title: Governance guardian can transfer guardianship to zero address
reason: This is trusted-role self-misconfiguration, and the guardian already holds stronger powers such as veto and arbitrary guardian execution; it is not a distinct adversarial vulnerability.
